'''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[square-front keyhole mounds|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * **Mound 1:** Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] * [[End-period kofun]] * [[Stone burial chambers]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[category:original pages]] [[Category:河田山古墳群]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[square-front keyhole mounds|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * **Mound 1:** Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] * [[End-period kofun]] * [[Stone burial chambers]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[square-front keyhole mounds|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * **Mound 1:** Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] * [[End-period kofun]] * [[Stone burial chambers]] == External Links == * Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] * [[End-period kofun]] * [[Stone burial chambers]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[square-front keyhole mounds|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * **Mound 1:** Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] * [[End-period kofun]] * [[Stone burial chambers]] == External Links == * Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * **Mound 1:** Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} - **Size:** Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. - **Artifacts:** Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. * **Mound 12:** Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} - **Size:** Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. - **Construction Date:** Late 7th century. * **Mound 36:** The largest square mound in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. - **Artifacts:** Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. - **Construction Date:** Mid-5th century. * **Mound 60:** The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. - **Size:** Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. - **Artifacts:** Iron swords, tubular jade beads. - **Construction Date:** Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * **By Car:** 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * **By Bus:** 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] * [[End-period kofun]] * [[Stone burial chambers]] == External Links == * Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[round mounds]] * 35 [[square mounds]] * 1 possible horizontal tomb * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] Kofun]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[ja:河田山古墳群]]

{{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[Category:河田山古墳群]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[Category:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河田山古墳群]]

Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]][[ja:河田山古墳群]]

{{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu|ja|小松市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoana tombs|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue ware]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-style burial chambers|横穴式石室]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|ja|小松駅}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite
