revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:小松市の歴史
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|qq=}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofuns in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[en:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[sn:awadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:小松市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|qq=}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofuns in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[sn:awadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:小松市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}}

{{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofuns in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[sn:awadayama 12-gō Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}}

Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute revision.comment: Bot: remove incorrect Shona (sn:) interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|qid=Q646898}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 [[commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == [[commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130455394]] Wikidata name: Kōdayama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of [[:en:Komatsu, Ishikawa|Komatsu]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from [[:en:Komatsu Station|Komatsu Station]] via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}} {{Expand Japanese}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Cave tomb|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from
