revision.text:
{{moved from|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun}} [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages]]

Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a {{moved from|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun}} [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. {{moved from|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun}} [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{moved from|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun}} [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station|1=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} {{moved from|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun}} [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station|1=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station|1=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station|1=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}} [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōdayama Kofun Group]][[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:240315 Koudayama Tomb No12.jpg|location={{ill|Komatsu|WD=Q646898|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}}} '''Kōdayama Kofun Cluster''' (河田山古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in the northeastern area of {{ill|Komatsu, Ishikawa|en|Komatsu, Ishikawa|WD=Q646898|lt=Komatsu|1=Komatsu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development. Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|WD=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station|1=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] * [[Late Kofun tumuli|終末期古墳]] * [[Fukiishi]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html Official page for Kōdayama Kofun Cluster] * [https://kanazawa-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/54214 Kanazawa University Archaeological Reports] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|河田山古墳群|version=100655120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:河田山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kōdayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q130455394}} [[Category:History of Komatsu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Ishikawa prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Green: Relocated and preserved mounds.]] == Key Features == * '''Mound 1:''' Square-front keyhole-shaped kofun discovered in 1977. Excavated in 1982–1983 by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association.{{Cite book|title=Report on the Initial Excavation of Kōdayama Mound 1|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|year=1983}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 25 m total length, rear mound width approx. 15 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Fragments of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|qid=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. * '''Mound 12:''' Square mound featuring an arched cut-stone horizontal chamber, relocated 30 m due to residential development.{{Cite book|title=History and Development of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} ** '''Size:''' Approx. 14.5 m width and length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, silver-decorated sword fittings. ** '''Construction Date:''' Late 7th century. * '''Mound 36:''' The largest square mound in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 17 m length, 3 m height. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron tools such as spades and axes, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. ** '''Construction Date:''' Mid-5th century. * '''Mound 60:''' The largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the cluster. ** '''Size:''' Approx. 50 m total length, rear mound diameter 28.5 m. ** '''Artifacts:''' Iron swords, tubular jade beads. ** '''Construction Date:''' Early 4th century. == Archaeological Park and Museum == The Kōdayama Archaeological Park includes Mounds 1, 9, and the relocated Mound 12. Nearby is the {{ill|Kaga Provincial Museum|ja|加賀国府ものがたり館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Provincial Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which showcases the reconstructed stone chamber of Mound 33 and other exhibits.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/R5_kokufumuseum_leaflet.pdf|title=Kaga Provincial Museum Brochure|publisher=Komatsu City|accessdate=2024-03-10}} == Research and Development Timeline == * 1977: Discovery of Mound 1 by the Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association. * 1982–1983: Excavation of Mound 1 by Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association. * 1986: Excavation expands to include Mounds 6, 7, and others. * 1987: The discovery of Japan's first arched stone chamber (Mound 12) is reported in national newspapers.{{Cite book|title=Reports on the Discovery of Kōdayama Mound 12|author=Ishikawa Archaeological Research Association|year=1987}} * 1992: Opening of the Kōdayama Archaeological Park and Museum. * 2022: Mounds 1, 9, and 12 designated as Komatsu City Historic Sites. * 2023: Museum renamed and reopened as the Kaga Provincial Museum. == Access == * '''By Car:''' 13 minutes from {{ill|Komatsu Station|en|Komatsu Station|qid=Q5359990|lt=Komatsu Station|1=Komatsu Station}} via National Route 305. * '''By Bus:''' 30 minutes from Komatsu Station on the Hokutetsu Kaga Bus line to "Kōdai," then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association|title=Report on Kōdayama Mound 1|year=1983}} * {{Cite book|author=Komatsu City Board of Education|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster Excavation Report|year=2020}} == Related Articles == * Most of the mounds were excavated and destroyed during the industrial development of the eastern Komatsu area in the late 1980s. Some notable features include the arched cut-stone horizontal burial chamber of Mound 12. == Overview == The Kōdayama Kofun Cluster originally consisted of 65 mounds, as per the Komatsu City official page.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.komatsu.lg.jp/soshiki/1052/13761/kodayama/index.html|title=Introduction to the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Official Website|accessdate=2024-03-17}} However, excavation reports by the Kanazawa University Archaeological Research Association suggest there may have been more mounds in the area.{{Cite book|title=Kōdayama Kofun Cluster|publisher=Komatsu City Board of Education|year=2020}} The cluster includes: * 2 [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofuns]] * 2 [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|square-front keyhole mounds]] * 24 [[Round Kofun|round mounds]] * 35 [[Square Kofun|square mounds]] * 1 possible [[Yokoanabo|Yokoana tomb]] * 3 unidentified types === Map of the Cluster === [[File:Overall of Koudayama kofun cluster.jpg|thumb|420px|Map of the Kōdayama Kofun Cluster.
Black: Existing mounds. Yellow: Mounds destroyed during residential development.
